data.content:
This is an **Android tombstone** from a .NET MAUI app. Let me identify the .NET runtime frames and attempt symbolication.

**Crash Summary:**
- **App**: `com.companyname.MauiTest` (arm64)
- **Signal**: SIGABRT — abort message: "not compatible"
- **Runtime**: Mono (`libmonosgen-2.0.so`)
- **BuildId**: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`

Frames #01–#07 are all in `libmonosgen-2.0.so`. Let me try to download debug symbols from the Microsoft symbol server and symbolicate them.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-addr2line llvm-symbolizer 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i binutils | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using ELF BuildId
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
SYMDIR="/tmp/sv-48bef33e441b435092a253ea42269eb4/symbols"
mkdir -p "$SYMDIR"

# Try the standard symbol server URL format for ELF binaries
# Format: https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/<filename>/elf-buildid-sym-<buildid>/<filename>
echo "=== Trying symbol server for libmonosgen-2.0.so ==="
curl -sfL -o "$SYMDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  && echo "Downloaded debug symbols" || echo "First URL format failed"

# Alternative format
curl -sfL -o "$SYMDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg2" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}ffffffff/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  && echo "Downloaded via alt format" || echo "Alt format failed"

# Another common format
curl -sfL -o "$SYMDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg3" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  && echo "Downloaded via elf-buildid format" || echo "elf-buildid format failed"

ls -la "$SYMDIR/"
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

data.reasoningText:
This is an Android tombstone/crash log (not an Apple .ips file), so the `apple-crash-symbolication` skill is not appropriate here. Let me analyze this crash log manually.

The crash is from a .NET MAUI app (`com.companyname.MauiTest`) on Android arm64. The key details:

1. **Signal**: SIGABRT (signal 6) - the app called abort()
2. **Abort message**: "not compatible"
3. **Library**: `libmonosgen-2.0.so` - this is the Mono runtime (used by .NET MAUI on Android)


4. **BuildId**: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`

The .NET runtime frames are frames #01 through #07, all in `libmonosgen-2.0.so`. To symbolicate this on Android, I need to locate the matching debug symbols for this specific build of the Mono runtime library, then use tools like `addr2line` or `llvm-symbolizer` from the Android NDK to map the crash addresses to actual function names and source locations.

The BuildId can help me find the right symbols—either from the NuGet package for the runtime (like `Microsoft.NETCore.App.Runtime.Mono.android-arm64`) or potentially from the Microsoft symbol server, which does serve ELF debug info using the BuildId in the URL format. I should check whether `addr2line` or `llvm-addr2line` is available on the system to help decode the stack trace.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-addr2line llvm-symbolizer 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i binutils | head -5

# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using ELF BuildId
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
SYMDIR="/tmp/sv-48bef33e441b435092a253ea42269eb4/symbols"
mkdir -p "$SYMDIR"

# Try the standard symbol server URL format for ELF binaries
# Format: https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/<filename>/elf-buildid-sym-<buildid>/<filename>
echo "=== Trying symbol server for libmonosgen-2.0.so ==="
curl -sfL -o "$SYMDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  && echo "Downloaded debug symbols" || echo "First URL format failed"

# Alternative format
curl -sfL -o "$SYMDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg2" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}ffffffff/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  && echo "Downloaded via alt format" || echo "Alt format failed"

# Another common format
curl -sfL -o "$SYMDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg3" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  && echo "Downloaded via elf-buildid format" || echo "elf-buildid format failed"

ls -la "$SYMDIR/"
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "which addr2line llvm-addr2line llvm-symbolizer 2>/dev/null; echo \"---\"; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i binutils | head -5", "description": "Check for symbolication tools", "initial_wait": 10}

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args:
{"command": "# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using ELF BuildId
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
SYMDIR="/tmp/sv-48bef33e441b435092a253ea42269eb4/symbols"
mkdir -p "$SYMDIR"

# Try the standard symbol server URL format for ELF binaries
# Format: https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/<filename>/elf-buildid-sym-<buildid>/<filename>
echo "=== Trying symbol server for libmonosgen-2.0.so ==="
curl -sfL -o "$SYMDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  && echo "Downloaded debug symbols" || echo "First URL format failed"

# Alternative format
curl -sfL -o "$SYMDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg2" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}ffffffff/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  && echo "Downloaded via alt format" || echo "Alt format failed"

# Another common format
curl -sfL -o "$SYMDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg3" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  && echo "Downloaded via elf-buildid format" || echo "elf-buildid format failed"

ls -la "$SYMDIR/"", "description": "Download debug symbols from MS symbol server", "initial_wait": 30}
